reflection.theme: The Ordinary Middle of the Year
reflection.quote: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
reflection.reading:
July 1st is a strange little hinge. The year is exactly half spent. No one throws a party for it, no one makes resolutions on it, and that's kind of the point. This is the ordinary middle — the part of the calendar where the version of you from January is either still showing up or has quietly stopped. Sobriety lives here, in the unglamorous middle. Not in the dramatic Day One, not in the anniversary chip. In the Wednesday.

When I was drinking, I couldn't stand the middle of anything. The middle of the year, the middle of the week, the middle of a conversation I wasn't interested in. I always needed the next thing — the weekend, the trip, the drink that would rearrange the room. Sobriety asked me to stop escaping the middle. To actually be in July, in a Wednesday, in this coffee, in this run, in this phone call with my mom.

What I found in the middle was a life. Not a highlight reel — a life. The small repeated actions that Durant, channeling Aristotle, was pointing at. Showing up isn't excellent because it looks impressive. It's excellent because it's what you're doing when no one is clapping. Half the year is behind you. The next half is built one Wednesday at a time.
reflection.meditation: What small, unglamorous action have I been showing up for — and what does it say about who I'm becoming?

affirmations: I am building a life in the ordinary days, not just the memorable ones.
Today I can be fully present in the middle of my week without needing to escape it.
I have agency over what I repeat, and what I repeat becomes who I am.

gratitude.prayer: Grateful for another Wednesday I remember. Grateful for the half of this year I've already walked through sober — the boring parts, the hard parts, the surprisingly good parts. Grateful for coffee that tastes like coffee, mornings that start clean, and a life that no longer requires an escape route.
gratitude.closing: Halfway through the year. Still here. Still showing up.
